title: How do Demoniac Think, Feel and Act ?  | BG 16.08 - 16.12 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: So, today we will be discussing from the 16th chapter of the Gita, text number 7 to 10.
So, the topic for today's discussion for the verses is, how do the demoniac think, feel and act?
Now, a question may come in our mind, why do we need to discuss or understand how do demoniac think?
We can as well be thinking about how the saintly people think, what their qualities are.
So, once there was a person who was a drunkard, alcoholic.
All kinds of bad qualities, you could call, was there in his character.
And no doubt the whole family life was in complete chaos.
He used to beat his wife. Financially, they were in a very bad state.
So, he had two sons.
So, they were brought up in this...
You know, circumstances.
So, one of them also became a drunkard.
And the second one, he studied quite well and eventually became an IAS officer.
So, both these brothers were interviewed.
The first one who became a drunkard, he was asked,
how did you become a drunkard?
Why did you become a drunkard?
So, he told that my father was a drunkard.
What else do you expect from me?
I have all along seen my father drinking, beating my mother, doing all kinds of these things.
So, what else do you expect from me?
Sangat, Sanjayate, Kama.
Based on the association we keep, the kind of people we associate with,
that kind of desires,
that kind of character we develop.
So, what else do you expect from me?
And quite in contrast to this brother, the other one became an IAS officer, very well educated.
So, he was asked that your brother is a drunkard, your father is a drunkard.
How did you, you know, became so successful in life?
So, he said, my father was a drunkard.
All along, I have seen him drinking.
What else do you expect me to become?
In other words, we can learn even from the negatives.
How not to be like them.
So, emphasis is we are going to discuss about how the demoniac think.
So, we should not think like them.
How do they act?
We should not act like them.
And if we also, we all are demoniac to different degrees.
It's not a binary logic.
You all agree with what I am saying?
It's not zero or one.
It's not that we have started chanting or becoming Krishna devotees, so we are not demoniac.
We still have demoniac tendencies.
We become conversant that these tendencies, these thought processes are from demoniac mentality.
So that we understand that and work on those qualities to overcome them and develop saintly qualities or godly qualities.
So, dedicated to his divine grace, Srila Prabhupada, inspiration behind all our activities, all the wonderful teachings, which is the basis of our discussion.

Now, this is true. You will all agree with this. We all have intrinsically four defects. What are the four defects we have?
Be it the most...
Most knowledgeable person in this world, most erudite, most brilliant person, a noble laureate, he will also have these four intrinsic defects. These are called defects of a conditioned soul.
And the four defects are, the four imperfections are imperfect senses. Our senses are imperfect. And we have knowledge acquiring senses.
We are acquiring knowledge from...

We are acquiring knowledge from the senses. And that's how we have built a world view. But the senses themselves are imperfect. To give you an example, a merchant who is trying to be honest, ethical, and not cheat his customers, but what if the weights what he is using have some defect, are imperfect.
Then even though his intention is to be...
Not cheat the customer, he ends up cheating the customer because the weights are imperfect.
The many people are involved in perceiving the world through the senses and getting a world view, they are not necessarily...
